summary: Iteration 47 - Undo/Redo Fix Verification - ALL TESTS PASSED. The useCanvasHistory hook rewrite using refs and the Ctrl+Z handler fix for isEditing check are working correctly.

Ctrl+Z_test.setup: Created AAA and BBB text elements, pressed Escape to commit

useCanvasHistory.js.changes: Uses useRef for historyRef and indexRef instead of useState to prevent stale closure issues. Deep copies via JSON.parse/stringify.
useCanvasHistory.js.key_lines: Lines 6-8: refs initialization, Line 11: JSON.parse(JSON.stringify(elements)) for deep copy

Editor.js_Ctrl+Z_handler.changes: Ctrl+Z/Y handler checks isEditing. If user is in contentEditable, browser handles native undo. Canvas-level undo only when NOT editing.
Editor.js_Ctrl+Z_handler.key_lines: Lines 353-359: isEditing check before calling handleUndo/handleRedo

lastLoadedPageRef.changes: Prevents history.reset() from firing on auto-save. History only resets on actual page navigation.
lastLoadedPageRef.key_lines: Lines 435-444: lastLoadedPageRef comparison to skip reset on same page

important_testing_notes: CRITICAL: After typing text, press ESCAPE to exit editing mode before testing Ctrl+Z. The text commits on blur/escape.
Clicking on empty canvas area creates a NEW text element (Word-like behavior) - this puts you in editing mode
When in editing mode (contentEditable has focus), Ctrl+Z performs browser native undo in the text field, NOT canvas-level undo
To test canvas-level undo via Ctrl+Z: 1) Type text, 2) Press Escape to commit and exit editing, 3) Press Ctrl+Z
critical_code_review_comments: VERIFIED: useCanvasHistory.js - Ref-based implementation correctly prevents stale closure issues that plagued useState version
VERIFIED: Editor.js Ctrl+Z handler (lines 353-359) - Correctly skips canvas undo when isEditing is true
VERIFIED: lastLoadedPageRef (lines 435-444) - Properly prevents history reset on auto-save
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 47 verified the Undo/Redo fix. Key insight: Clicking on empty canvas creates new text element and puts user in editing mode - this is by design (Word-like behavior). To properly test Ctrl+Z, always press Escape first to exit editing mode. Both Undo/Redo buttons and Ctrl+Z/Ctrl+Y keyboard shortcuts work correctly when user is NOT in editing mode.
